name: Headless UI
description: Completely unstyled, fully accessible UI components for React and Vue, designed to integrate beautifully with Tailwind CSS

description.value: Completely unstyled, fully accessible UI components for React and Vue, designed to integrate beautifully with Tailwind CSS

license.comment: Open source MIT license, maintained by Tailwind Labs

active-maintenance.comment: Active - 942 commits, 101 contributors, v2.2.9 released September 2025, maintained by Tailwind Labs

initial-release.comment: Announced October 6, 2020 with v0.2.0; v1.0 released in April 2021

framework.comment: Official packages for React (@headlessui/react v2.x) and Vue (@headlessui/vue v1.x)

styling-approach.comment: Completely unstyled components; designed to work with Tailwind CSS but compatible with any styling solution

component-model.comment: Headless component library providing behavior and accessibility without any default styles

typescript.comment: Written in TypeScript (95.4% of codebase), provides first-class type definitions

ssr-compatible.comment: Works with server-side rendering frameworks like Next.js, Nuxt, etc.

rsc-compatible.comment: Components require 'use client' directive in Next.js App Router; uses hooks (useState, useEffect) so cannot be server components

design-system.comment: Headless library with no design system; designed to be styled with Tailwind CSS or custom styles

dark-mode.comment: Unstyled components - dark mode is user's responsibility via Tailwind's dark: variant or custom CSS

theming-system.comment: No built-in theming system - completely unstyled, user applies their own theme

css-variables.comment: Components don't include CSS variables for theming; provides CSS variables only for positioning (--anchor-gap, etc.)

design-tokens.comment: No design tokens provided - headless library focused on behavior and accessibility

rtl-support.comment: Components work with RTL layouts; CSS positioning respects document direction

component-count.comment: 16 components: Button, Checkbox, Combobox, Dialog, Disclosure, Fieldset, Input, Listbox, Menu, Popover, Radio Group, Select, Switch, Tabs, Textarea, Transition

text-input.comment: Input component with accessible styling hooks and form integration

textarea.comment: Textarea component with accessible styling hooks and form integration

select.comment: Listbox (custom select) and native Select wrapper with keyboard navigation and ARIA support

multi-select.comment: Listbox supports multiple selection via 'multiple' prop with array values

combobox.comment: Full-featured Combobox with autocomplete, virtual scrolling, multiple selection, and custom value support

checkbox.comment: Checkbox component with indeterminate state, form integration, and accessible labels

radio.comment: Radio Group component with keyboard navigation, object values, and description support

switch-toggle.comment: Switch component for boolean toggle values with keyboard and form support

slider.comment: Not provided - use native HTML range input or third-party library

number-input.comment: Not provided as distinct component - use native HTML number input with Input wrapper

tag-input.comment: Not provided - can be built with Combobox multiple selection mode

date-picker.comment: Not provided; was mentioned in 2020 roadmap but not yet implemented

input-groups.comment: Fieldset component for grouping related form controls with accessible labels and descriptions

input-with-dropdown.comment: Combobox combines text input with dropdown; additional patterns can be composed

button-groups.comment: Not provided as distinct component - can be styled with Button components

split-button.comment: Not provided - can be composed using Button and Menu

table.comment: Not provided - use native HTML table or third-party libraries

data-table.comment: Not provided - use TanStack Table or similar

virtual-infinite-list.comment: Combobox includes virtual scrolling for large datasets; general virtualization not provided

card.comment: Not provided - layout component beyond primitive scope

alert.comment: Not provided as static alert component

toast-notification.comment: Not provided - use third-party toast libraries like react-hot-toast or sonner

modal-dialog.comment: Dialog component with focus trapping, inert background, keyboard support (Esc to close), and accessible labels

drawer-sheet.comment: Not provided as distinct primitive; Dialog can be styled as slide-out drawer

popover.comment: Floating panels with configurable positioning, grouping, backdrop, and animation support

tooltip.comment: Not provided - use Popover or third-party tooltip library

dropdown-menu.comment: Menu component with keyboard navigation, sections, headings, and separators

context-menu.comment: Not provided as right-click context menu; Menu is click/focus triggered

command-palette.comment: Not provided; can be composed with Dialog and Combobox

tabs.comment: Tabs with keyboard navigation, vertical orientation, manual activation, and controlled/uncontrolled modes

vertical-tabs.comment: Tabs component supports vertical orientation via 'vertical' prop with appropriate keyboard behavior

navigation-menu.comment: Not provided as distinct component; can be built with Menu and Popover

sidebar.comment: Not provided; can be built with Disclosure or Dialog

grid.comment: Not provided - CSS layout beyond primitive scope

stack.comment: Not provided - CSS layout beyond primitive scope

container.comment: Not provided - CSS layout beyond primitive scope

divider.comment: Not provided; Menu has MenuSeparator for dropdown contexts only

aspect-ratio.comment: Not provided - use Tailwind's aspect-ratio utilities

collapsible.comment: Disclosure component for expandable/collapsible content with keyboard support and animation

form-libraries.comment: No official bindings; works with any form library via standard controlled components

animation-libraries.comment: Built-in Transition component and CSS transition support; documented Framer Motion integration with 'static' prop

icon-libraries.comment: Heroicons by Tailwind Labs is the recommended icon library; examples in docs use Heroicons

date-libraries.comment: No date components, so no date library integrations

aria-compliant.comment: All components implement WAI-ARIA patterns with proper roles, states, and properties

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard support: arrow keys, Home/End, Enter/Space, Escape, typeahead character navigation

screen-reader-tested.comment: Components designed with screen reader accessibility in mind; over 3,000 lines of tests

focus-management.comment: Focus trapping in dialogs, automatic focus on first focusable element, focus restoration, inert background handling

wcag-level.comment: No specific WCAG level documented; follows WAI-ARIA authoring practices

accessibility-rating.comment: Good - WAI-ARIA compliant, full keyboard navigation, focus management, screen reader support; extensive test coverage

bundle-size-full.comment: 67.6 kB minified + gzipped for full @headlessui/react package

bundle-size-core.comment: Single package without separate core; tree-shaking reduces actual bundle size

tree-shakeable.comment: Package supports tree-shaking; import individual components to minimize bundle size

zero-runtime.comment: JavaScript runtime required for component behavior and accessibility

lazy-loading.comment: Components can be dynamically imported and code-split

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent - comprehensive API docs, live examples, keyboard shortcut tables, accessibility explanations, styling guides, animation tutorials

interactive-docs.comment: Documentation includes interactive component examples with live code

storybook.comment: No official Storybook; uses custom documentation site

figma-kit.comment: No official Figma kit from Tailwind Labs; community-created alternatives exist (Headless Design System Starter, etc.)

cli-generator.comment: No CLI tool; install via npm/pnpm and import components directly

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~3.1M weekly downloads for @headlessui/react as of December 2025

commercial-support.comment: Open source MIT license; no commercial support tier. Tailwind Labs offers paid Tailwind UI and Catalyst products that use Headless UI

style-override.comment: Components accept className and style props; expose data-* attributes for state-based styling

compound-components.comment: Extensive compound component pattern (e.g., Menu, MenuButton, MenuItems, MenuItem)

render-props.comment: Components expose state via render props (e.g., open, active, selected, disabled) for conditional rendering

slots.comment: Uses compound components and render props rather than slots pattern

unstyled-mode.comment: Entirely unstyled by default - headless component library providing only behavior and accessibility
